name: api-security-testing
description: API security testing workflow for REST and GraphQL APIs covering authentication, authorization, rate limiting, input validation, and security best practices.
capabilities: Guides through 7-phase API security testing workflow
Tests rate limiting and input validation
Covers both REST and GraphQL API security
Provides copy-paste prompts for each testing phase
Includes API security checklist and quality gates
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 26 external_commands patterns and 1 cryptographic issue, but all findings are false positives. The backticks detected are Markdown code block delimiters, not Ruby shell execution. The skill is documentation-only with no executable code, network calls, or dangerous patterns. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: API security testing requires systematic coverage of authentication, authorization, and injection vulnerabilities. This workflow guides you through seven comprehensive testing phases using specialized security skills.
userTitle: Test API Security with Structured Workflows
limitations: Workflow documentation only - requires separate security testing skills to execute
Does not perform actual security tests independently
Focused on web APIs - not suitable for binary or network protocol testing
Requires user to manually invoke referenced skills for each phase

useCases.targetUser: Bug bounty hunters and security researchers
useCases.title: Bug Bounty API Testing
useCases.description: Security researchers testing API endpoints for vulnerabilities in bug bounty programs

useCases.title: Development Team Security Review
useCases.description: Development teams validating API security before production deployment

useCases.title: Security Audit Workflow
useCases.description: Consultants performing comprehensive API security assessments for clients

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: API Discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @api-fuzzing-bug-bounty to discover all API endpoints. Enumerate endpoints, document API methods, identify parameters, map data flows, and review available documentation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new API security assessment with no prior knowledge

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Authentication Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @broken-authentication to test API authentication mechanisms. Test API key validation, JWT token handling, OAuth2 flows, token expiration, and refresh token security.
promptTemplates.scenario: Validating authentication implementation after API discovery

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Authorization Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @idor-testing to test API authorization controls. Test object-level authorization, function-level access, role-based permissions, privilege escalation paths, and multi-tenant isolation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Testing for IDOR and access control vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Expert: GraphQL Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @api-fuzzing-bug-bounty to test GraphQL endpoint security. Test introspection settings, query depth limits, query complexity, batch query handling, and field suggestion exposure.

bestPractices: Complete all seven phases systematically for comprehensive coverage
Document all findings with reproduction steps and severity ratings
Test both happy paths and edge cases for each security control
antiPatterns: Skipping phases based on assumptions about the API
Testing without proper authorization from API owners
Focusing only on automated tests without manual verification

faq.question: What skills do I need to use this workflow?
faq.answer: This workflow references api-fuzzing-bug-bounty, broken-authentication, idor-testing, sql-injection-testing, and api-security-best-practices. Each phase invokes specific skills for targeted testing.

faq.question: Can I use this for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, Phase 6 specifically covers GraphQL security testing including introspection, query depth, complexity analysis, and batch query vulnerabilities.

faq.question: How long does a complete API security assessment take?
faq.answer: Assessment time varies by API complexity. Small APIs (10-20 endpoints) may take 2-4 hours. Larger APIs can require full days across multiple testing sessions.

faq.question: Is this suitable for automated security scanning?
faq.answer: This workflow is designed for manual security testing with AI assistance. For automated scanning, consider dedicated security scanning tools in addition to this workflow.

faq.question: What authorization do I need before testing?
faq.answer: Only test APIs you own or have explicit written permission to test. Unauthorized security testing may violate terms of service and applicable laws.

faq.question: How should I document vulnerabilities found?
faq.answer: Document each vulnerability with: description, affected endpoint, reproduction steps, potential impact, severity rating, and recommended remediation. Include evidence like request/response samples.
